2604.15842.title: Disentangling Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs: A Methodological Investigation of Internal Mechanisms
2604.15842.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities, yet their internal mechanisms for handling reasoning-intensive tasks remain underexplored. To advance the understanding of model-internal processing mechanisms, we present an investigation of how LLMs perform arithmetic operations by examining internal mechanisms during task execution. Using early decoding, we trace how next-token predictions are constructed across layers. Our experiments reveal that while the models recognize arithmetic tasks early, correct result generation occurs only in the final layers. Notably, models proficient in arithmetic exhibit a clear division of labor between attention and MLP modules, where attention propagates input information and MLP modules aggregate it. This division is absent in less proficient models. Furthermore, successful models appear to process more challenging arithmetic tasks functionally, suggesting reasoning capabilities beyond factual recall.
2604.15842.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）展現了令人印象深刻的能力，但它們在處理推理密集型任務的內部機制仍然未被充分探索。為了促進對模型內部處理機制的理解，我們進行了一項調查，研究LLMs如何通過檢查任務執行過程中的內部機制來執行算術運算。通過早期解碼，我們追蹤了下一個標記預測是如何在各層之間構建的。我們的實驗顯示，儘管模型早期就能識別算術任務，但正確結果的生成僅發生在最後幾層。值得注意的是，擅長算術的模型在注意力和多層感知器（MLP）模塊之間顯示出明確的分工，其中注意力傳遞輸入信息，而MLP模塊則進行聚合。這種分工在較不擅長的模型中是缺失的。此外，成功的模型似乎在功能上能夠處理更具挑戰性的算術任務，這表明其推理能力超越了事實回憶。
2604.15842.author: Tanja Baeumel et.al.
2604.15842.authors: Tanja Baeumel, Josef van Genabith, Simon Ostermann
